sonnet_review.reason: I flagged the full sentence including 'heated clash' as spin. Sonnet correctly identifies 'heated' as a subjective, emotionally charged adjective.
I flagged this same word as part of a broader detection about 'extraordinary' and 'even more shocking' being subjective characterizations.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Powell's rebuke of Trump was in some ways even more shocking
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged this same text. The categorization as 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact' is slightly different from my 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' but both are defensible.
I flagged this exact phrase as spin with loaded, interpretive language that frames Powell heroically.
I flagged 'stunning' as a subjective, sensational characterization by the author.
I did not flag this specific phrase, but upon review it does inject the author's subjective assessment into the reporting. It's a minor but defensible detection.
I did not flag this subheading, but 'emboldened' does attribute an internal state to Powell and carries an editorial, admiring connotation.
I flagged this same phrase under 'Slant,' noting that 'spectacularly' adds dramatic spin and the framing presupposes Trump is trying to rid the Fed of independence.

sonnet_review.biasedText: Economists, academics and former Fed officials broadly agree that safeguarding Fed independence is necessary for the soundness of the US economy.
sonnet_review.reason: I flagged this same sentence. Sonnet's categorization as 'Elite vs. Populist Bias' is different from my 'Opinion Statements Presented as Fact' but the appeal to elite authority without dissenting views is a valid framing.

gpt_review.biasedText: The gloves are off in the heated clash between Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and President Donald Trump.
gpt_review.reason: I flagged this exact sentence as spin with sensational, metaphorical language that frames the situation as a fight.

gpt_review.biasedText:
Powell
is rebuke of Trump was in some ways even more shocking.
gpt_review.reason: I flagged this same text for its subjective characterization with 'even more shocking.'
I flagged this exact phrase as interpretive, loaded language framing Powell heroically.

gpt_review.biasedText: It enables the Fed to do its core mission: making jobs plentiful and keeping prices in check, even when politically imprudent.
gpt_review.reason: I did not flag this specific sentence, but upon review, characterizing Fed independence as definitively enabling 'jobs plentiful' and 'keeping prices in check' does present a contested claim as established fact.

gpt_review.biasedText: Economists, academics and former Fed officials broadly agree that safeguarding Fed independence is necessary for the soundness of the US economy.
gpt_review.reason: I flagged this same sentence for presenting a broad consensus claim without specific sources and framing only one side as expert consensus.

gpt_review.biasedText: Trump has asked for more. Trump has said the United States should have rates that are among the lowest in the world and that he should have a say in rate decisions.
gpt_review.reason: This appears to be a largely factual summary of Trump's stated positions. While 'asked for more' could be slightly editorial, this is substantially an accurate description of Trump's own public statements and is not significantly spun.

gpt_review.biasedText:
The Fed has been forthcoming about the renovation
is cost overruns and its collaboration with other agencies, even posting an FAQ on its website with all those details. The Fed last summer also provided a guided tour of the renovation to a handful of reporters.
gpt_review.reason: I did not flag this, but upon review, the word 'forthcoming' and the emphasis on transparency without balancing with the allegations does favor the Fed's position over the administration's claims.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: The gloves are off in the heated clash between Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell and President Donald Trump.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet flagged 'heated clash' but missed the broader sensational framing of 'the gloves are off' as a dramatic, fight-metaphor opening.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText:
In his stunning two-minute video Sunday night, Powell characterized the Trump administration
is potential prosecution as a threat against the livelihoods of the American public to serve Trump
is whims.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: Sonnet flagged 'stunning' but missed 'Trump's whims' which is a loaded, dismissive framing of the president's motivations.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText:
Trump
is relentless pressure campaign over the past year
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: 'Relentless' is a loaded, interpretive adjective characterizing Trump's actions negatively beyond neutral description.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: For years, Powell has remained mum even as Trump called him every name and used every insult in the book, repeatedly threatening to fire him.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: 'Every name and used every insult in the book' is hyperbolic language that exaggerates to create sympathetic framing of Powell and negative framing of Trump.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText:
the Trump administration
is attack on its independence
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The author adopts Powell's characterization of the administration's legal actions as an 'attack' on Fed independence as though it is fact.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: Because Powell viewed the potential prosecution as an existential threat that could forever politicize the Fed, undermine its credibility and upset global markets and the economy.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The author presents Powell's internal reasoning as established fact without direct attribution.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: The Trump administration also has a tough case to prove against Powell.
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: The author states as fact that the case is 'tough to prove,' an editorial judgment favoring Powell's position.

sonnet_false_negatives.biasedText: the latest salvo in the Trump-Powell fight
sonnet_false_negatives.reason: 'Salvo' is militaristic, sensational language framing the situation as combat.

As extraordinary as a criminal investigation of a sitting Fed chair is, Powell
